keyPoints: A matrix can be represented as a polynomial in its own variables by creating a companion matrix.
The Cayley-Hamilton theorem states that every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation.
Eigenvalues are scalar values that represent how much a linear transformation changes a vector.
The characteristic polynomial of a linear transformation can be used to find its eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
The minimal polynomial of a linear transformation is the smallest polynomial that annihilates the transformation.
The null space of a linear transformation is the set of all eigenvectors corresponding to eigenvalues of zero.
